diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Massive dilute urine output is the cardinal feature of an inability to concentrate urine.
Osmotic diuresis from hyperglycaemia also causes polyuria — must be excluded by glucose/urinalysis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense polydipsia with specific craving for ice-cold water

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A characteristic craving for very cold water is classically associated with diabetes insipidus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute altered mental status after fluid restriction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confusion/obtundation with massive free-water loss indicates a rising serum sodium drawing water from neurons — a life-threatening red flag.
Deterioration specifically on fluid restriction unmasks the inability to conserve water.
Colourless urine reflects extreme dilution; observable by the patient/carer and admissible in telehealth, though not a substitute for measured specific gravity.
Nocturnal polyuria is consistent with a persistent concentrating defect but is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypovolaemic shock signs — tachycardia (HR 125), postural hypotension (BP 88/52), delayed capillary refill

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective circulatory collapse — clinician-measured and sealed; the AI Doctor infers severe dehydration from the history and altered consciousness, not from a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Parched oral mucosa and severely reduced skin turgor (tenting)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Examination signs of profound volume depletion — require a clinician's hands, sealed as answer key.

symptoms.symptom_name: Generalised hyperreflexia with global disorientation, no focal deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neurological irritability of hypernatraemia; a clinician examination finding, sealed.

investigation_details.expected_result: Fixed low specific gravity ~1.001 (maximally dilute) despite clinical shock
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Failure to document inappropriately dilute urine misses the key evidence of a concentrating defect.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Maximally dilute urine in the face of hypovolaemic shock is near-diagnostic of a concentrating defect; a clinician/equipment measurement, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent glycosuria and normal capillary blood glucose

investigation_details.investigation_name: Bedside urinalysis for glucose and capillary blood glucose
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without excluding hyperglycaemia, a diabetic hyperglycaemic crisis could be wrongly assumed or missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent glycosuria with a normal glucose excludes a hyperglycaemic osmotic diuresis as the cause.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated serum sodium and osmolality confirming hypernatraemic dehydration
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The degree of hypernatraemia guides the rate of safe correction; unavailable in telehealth, obtained only in hospital.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the biochemical basis of the encephalopathy; a hospital blood test the AI Doctor cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: No lithium use and no recent cranial trauma

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Screens common secondary causes (lithium-induced nephrogenic DI, post-traumatic central DI) but does not change the acute emergency management.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Profound dilute polyuria, ice-water polydipsia, inappropriately dilute urine and hypernatraemia together characterise decompensated diabetes insipidus.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum sodium, urea, electrolytes and osmolality
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the hypernatraemia and guides the safe rate of correction — a hospital emergency test, arranged by escalation, not by the AI Doctor.
Rapidly excludes a hyperglycaemic crisis as the cause of polyuria and altered consciousness.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Paired serum/urine osmolality and water-deprivation / desmopressin testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms diabetes insipidus and distinguishes central from nephrogenic subtype — an inpatient/specialist work-up done only after the acute emergency is stabilised, never in real time via telehealth.
